verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease released into prostatic fluid and seminal plasma; it has no transmembrane domain, GPI anchor, or documented wash-resistant covalent tether to a surface TM partner. It acts in the extracellular/luminal milieu (notably cleaving pro-PSA) and circulates as a soluble protein. Any transient interaction with surface substrates is reversible enzyme-substrate engagement, not stable anchoring. Imaging/therapeutic interest exists, but the protein body itself is not stably displayed on the outer leaflet.
